title: Best Free Chordify Alternatives (2026 Guide)
description: Looking for a free Chordify alternative without monthly paywalls, song limits, or banner ads? Compare the best web-based chord AI tools for guitarists and producers.
author: Abhinav Vaidya
html:
When learning new songs on guitar, piano, or ukulele, Chordify has long been one of the most recognizable web platforms for automatic chord recognition. By searching YouTube links or uploading audio files, musicians get an automated animated chord sheet synced to playback.

However, over recent years, Chordify's free tier has become increasingly restrictive. Key essential tools—such as transposing audio, pitch shifting, downloading MIDI files, editing chord voicings, and isolating vocals—are locked behind a monthly paid subscription (Chordify Premium). Furthermore, free users are often bombarded with intrusive video and banner ads.

If you are looking for a powerful, web-based alternative that gives you premium chord detection features without subscription paywalls, Guitariz Studio is the leading free alternative in 2026.

What to Look for in a Free Chordify Alternative

Before comparing platforms, here are the non-negotiable features modern musicians need from an AI chord transcribing tool:

  1. Unrestricted Chord Complexity: Full support for 7th chords, minor 7ths, diminished, augmented, and slash chords (e.g., C/E, G/B).
  2. Instant Key Transposition: Pitch-shift audio up or down in real time to match your capo or vocal range.
  3. MIDI Export: Instant 1-click export of detected harmonic progressions to .mid files for DAW import.
  4. Vocal & Instrumental Stem Filtering: AI-driven source separation to remove vocals for cleaner chord extraction on dense mixes.
  5. No Registration or Paywall: Instant browser analysis without forced account creation.

1. Guitariz Studio (100% Free & Open-Source)

Guitariz Studio is a modern, open-source digital workbench built specifically for musicians, songwriters, and producers.

Key Advantages over Chordify:

  • 100% Free & No Paywalls: Every single feature—including 6-stem separation, key transposition, tap tempo, and MIDI export—is completely free for unlimited tracks.
  • 1-Click MIDI Export: Export any detected or transposed chord progression straight to Standard MIDI (.mid) format to drag into Ableton Live, FL Studio, Logic Pro, or Cubase.
  • Integrated Vocal Remover: Uses state-of-the-art Meta Demucs neural networks to isolate instrumental backing tracks before analysis, drastically boosting detection accuracy on vocal-heavy tracks.
  • Interactive Fretboard & Diagrams: View dynamic 24-fret guitar fingerings, 3D neck views, and chord voicings in sync with audio playback.
  • Privacy-First Architecture: Your uploaded audio is processed statelessly in memory and automatically discarded after analysis.

2. Moises AI

Moises is a popular mobile and web platform primarily focused on AI stem separation and speed/pitch adjustment. It also includes an automatic chord detection feature.

The Catch:

While Moises excels at stem isolation, its free tier restricts users to 5 audio separations per month and caps uploaded audio tracks at 5 minutes in length. Advanced MIDI export and high-fidelity Demucs models require a paid subscription.

3. Yamaha Chord Tracker (Mobile App Only)

Yamaha offers a dedicated mobile app (Chord Tracker) that automatically analyzes audio stored locally on your iOS or Android device.

The Catch:

Chord Tracker is strictly a mobile app—it lacks a web version, cannot analyze YouTube audio directly, and does not support MIDI export or multi-instrument stem separation.

Feature Comparison Matrix

Feature Guitariz Studio Chordify (Free Tier) Moises (Free Tier)
Pricing 100% Free Free / $6.99/mo Free / $3.99/mo
Chord Transposition ✅ Included ❌ Locked (Premium) ✅ Included
MIDI Export ✅ Included ❌ Locked (Premium) ❌ Locked (Premium)
Stem Isolation ✅ Meta Demucs ❌ Not available ⚠️ 5 songs/mo limit
BPM & Key Finder ✅ Included ✅ Included ✅ Included
Ads & Paywalls ❌ None ⚠️ Heavy Ads ⚠️ Upgrade Prompts

How to Get Started with Guitariz

Ready to transcribe your favorite tracks without paywalls?

  1. Head over to Guitariz Chord AI.
  2. Upload your MP3, WAV, or audio track (or try dedicated micro-tools like Audio Key Finder or BPM Detector).
  3. View your synchronized chord timeline, transpose to any key, and click Export MIDI to drag the progression directly into your DAW!

Disclaimer: Chordify, Moises, and Yamaha are registered trademarks of their respective owners. Guitariz Studio is an independent open-source platform and is not affiliated with, sponsored by, or endorsed by these companies.

title: Export AI Chords to MIDI for Ableton & FL Studio
description: Learn how to instantly convert audio songs to Standard MIDI files (.mid) and import detected chord progressions directly into Ableton Live, FL Studio, or Logic Pro.
author: Abhinav Vaidya
html:
Transcribing chords from an MP3 or YouTube video is only half the battle for modern music producers. Once you know that a song uses a Cmaj7 - Am7 - Dm9 - G13 progression, manually plotting those notes block-by-block into your Digital Audio Workstation (DAW) piano roll can be time-consuming.

With the release of Guitariz Studio 1.7.0, you can now detect harmonic progressions from any audio file and export them directly to Standard MIDI (.mid) files with 1 click.

In this step-by-step tutorial, we will show you how to detect chords from an audio track and drag-and-drop the exported MIDI file straight into Ableton Live, FL Studio, Logic Pro, or Cubase.

Step 1: Detect Chords with Guitariz Chord AI

  1. Open Guitariz Chord AI in your browser.
  2. Upload your audio file (MP3, WAV, FLAC, M4A).
  3. If the track has heavy vocals, toggle Vocal Filter to isolate the instrumental backing before analysis.
  4. Once the analysis completes, inspect your detected key signature, tempo (BPM), and interactive chord timeline.

Step 2: (Optional) Transpose or Simplify Progression

Before exporting, you can customize how the MIDI notes will be generated:

  • Transpose Key: Use the Transpose slider to pitch-shift the chord progression up or down by up to 6 semitones (e.g., converting a track from F# Major to C Major). The exported MIDI will inherit your exact transposed notes.
  • Simple Chords Mode: Toggle Simple Chords if you want clean triads (e.g., C instead of Cmaj9#11) for simpler arrangement building blocks.

Step 3: Export the MIDI File

  1. Click the Export MIDI button located at the top right of the Chord AI player.
  2. Review the preview summary modal.
  3. Click Download MIDI. A clean Standard MIDI Format 0 file (.mid) will be saved instantly to your computer.

Step 4: Import into Your DAW

Importing into Ableton Live:

  1. Open your Ableton Live project.
  2. Drag the downloaded .mid file from your File Explorer / Finder directly onto an MIDI Track.
  3. Load a virtual synth or piano instrument (such as Grand Piano or Wavetable) onto the track.
  4. Ableton will automatically place the exact chord blocks with proper note durations and timing.

Importing into FL Studio:

  1. Open FL Studio and create a new Instrument Channel (e.g., FLEX or Keyscape).
  2. Drag the .mid file directly into the Channel Rack or open the Piano Roll (F7) and select File -> Import MIDI file.
  3. Set import options to Realign events and click OK.

Importing into Logic Pro & Cubase:

  1. Drag the .mid file onto a Software Instrument track in your main Arrangement view.
  2. Assign your preferred VST or AU plugin instrument.

Why Exporting MIDI Speeds Up Production

  • Instant Sampling & Remixing: Quickly harmonize new melodies or basslines over an existing song's chord structure.
  • Custom Voicing Control: Edit octave spreads, voice-leading, and inversion notes in your DAW piano roll.
  • Layering Synth Pads: Instantly trigger lush Omnisphere or Serum synth pads using AI-extracted harmonies.

Start Exporting MIDI Today

Try out the 1-click MIDI Export feature right now on Guitariz Chord AI or generate custom progressions with the Chord Progression Generator.

Disclaimer: Ableton, FL Studio, Logic Pro, Cubase, Serum, and Omnisphere are registered trademarks of their respective owners. Guitariz Studio is an independent open-source platform.

title: Moises vs Guitariz: Free 6-Stem AI Separation
description: Compare Moises AI and Guitariz Vocal Remover & Stem Separator. Learn which free audio splitter delivers better acapella extraction and multi-track isolation.
author: Abhinav Vaidya
html:
AI-driven audio source separation has fundamentally transformed how producers remix tracks, how singers practice acapellas, and how instrumentalists dissect complex song arrangements.

By analyzing mixed stereo audio files (MP3, WAV, FLAC), modern neural networks can split songs into distinct isolated channels—separating vocals, drums, basslines, guitars, and keyboards.

Two of the most widely discussed platforms for online stem separation in 2026 are Moises.ai and Guitariz Studio. In this breakdown, we compare both tools on isolation quality, speed, pricing, and limitations.

1. Moises.ai Overview

Moises is one of the pioneer commercial applications for AI audio separation. Available across iOS, Android, and web browsers, Moises offers a suite of tools including pitch shifting, tempo change, chord detection, and stem separation.

Moises Strengths:

  • Smooth mobile app user interface.
  • Smart metronome with count-in generation.
  • Lyric sync for karaoke practice.

Moises Limitations (Free Tier):

  • 5 Songs / Month Limit: Free accounts can only separate up to 5 tracks per month.
  • 5-Minute Audio Cap: Uploaded audio tracks longer than 5 minutes are truncated.
  • Standard Resolution: High-fidelity separation models (Hi-Fi) are restricted to paid subscribers ($3.99/mo or $39.99/yr).
  • Mandatory Account Registration: Requires signing up with an email address before processing any audio.

2. Guitariz Studio Overview

Guitariz Studio is a free, web-based open-source digital workbench powered by Meta Research's Demucs v4 deep learning architecture.

Guitariz Strengths:

  • 100% Free & Unlimited: No monthly track limits, no account registration, and no song duration caps.
  • State-of-the-Art Demucs Engine: Employs Meta's hybrid transformer model to split audio into up to 6 clean stems (Vocals, Drums, Bass, Guitar, Piano, and Other).
  • Dedicated Micro-Tools: Instant 1-click access to dedicated single-purpose tools like the Vocal Remover, Key Finder, and BPM Detector.
  • Full DAW Export: Download isolated stems as uncompressed 24-bit WAV files or high-bitrate MP3s directly into your DAW.
  • Integrated Theory Lab: View the detected musical key, tempo, and synchronized chord progressions alongside your separated stems.

Direct Feature Comparison

Feature Guitariz Studio Moises (Free Tier)
Monthly Track Limit Unlimited 5 Songs / Month
Account Required ❌ No registration required ✅ Email required
Max Song Duration No Limit 5 Minutes
Stem Extraction Models Meta Demucs 4-Stem & 6-Stem Standard 4-Stem
Export Formats WAV / MP3 MP3 (WAV locked to Premium)
In-Browser DAW Mixer ✅ Mute, Solo, Volume per stem ✅ Included
Pricing 100% Free $3.99/month for Premium

Performance & Audio Quality Comparison

When extracting vocals or isolating basslines, bleed (artifacts where drums or guitars leak into the vocal channel) is the main indicator of AI quality.

  • Acapella Extraction: Both platforms produce exceptionally clean vocal tracks on modern studio recordings. On dense rock or heavy metal tracks with distorted guitars, Guitariz's Demucs engine maintains clearer vocal transients with fewer phase artifacts.
  • Bass & Low-End Separation: Guitariz isolates sub-bass frequencies and bass guitar transients with crisp definition, making it ideal for bass transcribers and hip-hop sample producers.

Summary: Which Tool Should You Use?

  • Choose Moises if you primarily work on a smartphone and need a mobile app with smart click tracks for live rehearsals.
  • Choose Guitariz Studio if you want unlimited, high-fidelity stem separation directly in your desktop or laptop browser without monthly track caps, credit card forms, or account sign-ups.

Try it out today: Guitariz Vocal Remover & Stem Separator.

Disclaimer: Moises is a registered trademark of Moises Systems Inc. Guitariz Studio is an independent open-source platform and is not affiliated with, sponsored by, or endorsed by Moises.

title: Best Free Chord AI Alternatives (2026)
description: Looking for a free alternative to Chord AI? Compare the best web-based and desktop platforms to detect chords from MP3 and YouTube online.
author: Abhinav Vaidya
html:
If you are looking to transcribe your favorite songs, Chord AI is one of the most popular mobile tools available. However, because it requires installing an app and locks premium chords (like 7ths, diminished, and slash chords) behind subscription plans, many musicians look for a free online alternative.

In this guide, we will compare the top web-based chord detectors and explain how you can extract chords from MP3s and YouTube videos completely for free.

1. Guitariz Chord AI (Web-Based & 100% Free)

If you want the power of Chord AI without downloading any apps or typing in credit card details, Guitariz Chord AI is the best online alternative.

Why it stands out:

  • Stateless and Private: Your files are processed entirely in memory and are deleted immediately after analysis. Your data is 100% private.
  • Interactive Chord Diagrams: View real-time guitar fingerings and diagrams synced with the playback of your song.
  • Transposition Controls: Pitch-shift the audio and transpose chords instantly to match your preferred key or vocal range.
  • Detailed Confidence Scores: View an analytical graph displaying how confident the neural network is on each chord detection.

2. Chordify

Chordify is a well-known chord transcription website. It has a large catalog of pre-analyzed YouTube tracks and allows you to upload your own files.

The Catch:

While the basic version is free, it restricts key features behind a monthly premium paywall. In the free version, you cannot transpose songs, edit wrong chords, or download MIDI files, and the interface is heavily cluttered with banner ads.

3. AnthemScore (Desktop Software)

AnthemScore is professional-grade desktop software that uses machine learning to transcribe audio files into full sheet music and MIDI.

The Catch:

Unlike browser-based tools, AnthemScore requires downloading and installing software on a PC or Mac. It is also not free—it offers a 30-day trial, after which you must purchase a lifetime license starting at $29.

Which one should you choose?

If you are looking for professional, sheet-music-grade transcribing for composition, a desktop tool like AnthemScore is powerful. However, if you are a guitarist or keyboardist looking to quickly play along with an MP3, WAV, or YouTube video, a free online app like Guitariz Chord AI gives you premium features with absolutely no friction.

title: How to Isolate Bass & Vocals with Demucs AI
description: Learn how to split audio stems and isolate bass, vocals, and drums using the state-of-the-art Demucs AI models for free in your browser.
author: Abhinav Vaidya
html:
Whether you want to create a high-quality karaoke backing track, isolate a bassline to transcribe it, or sample a clean vocal acapella, stem separation AI has revolutionized music production.

At the center of this revolution is Demucs—a deep learning model developed by Meta Research that splits audio files into distinct tracks. In this guide, we’ll show you how to use Demucs for free in your browser.

What is Demucs?

Demucs stands for Deep Extractor for Music Sources. Unlike traditional EQ-based filters (which cut highs and lows and leave muddy artifacts), Demucs uses a neural network trained on thousands of multitrack recordings to reconstruct separate channels from scratch.

It can split a stereo track into up to 6 stems:

  1. Vocals (Clean acapella)
  2. Drums (Isolated percussion)
  3. Bass (Low-end bass guitar or synth)
  4. Guitar (Electric and acoustic guitars)
  5. Piano (Acoustic and electric pianos)
  6. Other (Synthesizers, brass, strings, and effects)

How to Use Demucs Free Online

To run Demucs on a local computer, you usually need a powerful GPU, Python familiarity, and command-line knowledge. To make this accessible to everyone, we have deployed Demucs models directly to the web.

Here is how to split your first track using the Guitariz Stem Separator:

Step 1: Upload Your Audio File

Drag and drop any MP3, WAV, or FLAC file into the extractor box. To ensure optimal results, try to use high-quality audio files (320kbps MP3 or lossless WAV).

Step 2: Choose Your Stem Model

  • If you just want an acapella or karaoke track, use the Vocal Splitter (2-Stem). It splits the audio into pure Vocals and a combined Instrumental backing track.
  • If you want individual instruments (like isolating just the bass or piano), use the Stem Separator (6-Stem).

Step 3: Process and Mix

Click the Separate button. The server will run the Demucs pipeline and return the individual tracks. You can use the built-in browser mixer to mute, solo, and adjust the volume of each instrument in real-time.

Step 4: Download Stems

Once you are happy with the mix, download the individual stems as high-quality WAV files for import into your DAW (like Ableton, FL Studio, or Logic Pro).

Practical Uses for Musicians

  • Drummers: Solo the drums to study complex fills, or mute the drums to play along with the song.
  • Vocalists: Isolate the vocals to hear the exact pitch and vocal inflections of the singer.
  • Producers: Create high-quality bootleg remixes by sampling isolated hooks without background noise.

title: Introduction to Indian Ragas on Guitar
description: Master the sound of Indian Classical Music on the guitar. Learn the ten Thaats, Raga Bilawal, Raga Yaman, and how to configure scales.
author: Abhinav Vaidya
html:
Indian Classical Music is a rich, modal musical system that spans thousands of years. While Western music is heavily focused on chord progressions and harmony, Indian classical music focuses on melody and microtones.

Translating this sound to the guitar can open up new sonic palettes for your solos, fusion projects, and acoustic playing. In this guide, we will break down the absolute basics of Indian Ragas on the guitar.

1. The Concept of Swaras (Notes)

In Indian music, notes are called Swaras. Instead of C-D-E-F-G-A-B, the Indian solfege system (Sargam) uses:

  • Sa (Tonic / Root note)
  • Re (Second)
  • Ga (Third)
  • Ma (Fourth)
  • Pa (Fifth)
  • Dha (Sixth)
  • Ni (Seventh)

Unlike Western music, where the root note changes when you modulate keys, the note Sa is always your fixed root. You select a drone key (e.g., E), and all other notes are played relative to E.

2. The 10 Thaats (Parent Scales)

In Hindustani classical music, ragas are categorized under 10 parent scales called Thaats. Think of these as the Indian equivalent to the 7 diatonic modes.

Here are two common Thaats you can play on your guitar today:

Thaat Bilawal (The Major Scale)

This is identical to the Western Major scale (Ionian Mode). All notes are natural.

  • Sargam: Sa Re Ga Ma Pa Dha Ni
  • Formula: 1 2 3 4 5 6 7

Thaat Yaman / Kalyan (The Lydian Mode)

This scale uses a sharp 4th note (Teevra Ma). It creates an ethereal, meditative sound.

  • Sargam: Sa Re Ga Ma# Pa Dha Ni
  • Formula: 1 2 3 #4 5 6 7

To visualize how these parent scales lay out across the guitar fretboard, check out our interactive Indian Raga Theory Lab.

3. Adapting Ragas on the Fretboard

Playing a Raga is more than just running up and down a scale. A Raga has specific rules:

  • Arohana: The ascending path (the notes you are allowed to play going up).
  • Avarohana: The descending path (the notes you play going down).
  • Vadi: The most important, emphasized note in the Raga.
  • Samvadi: The second most important note (usually a perfect fifth or fourth away from the Vadi).

For example, in Raga Bhupali (a beautiful pentatonic scale), you are not allowed to play the 4th (Ma) or 7th (Ni) notes. The Arohana and Avarohana are identical: Sa - Re - Ga - Pa - Dha - Sa'.

Tips for Guitarists

  1. Use a Drone: Since there are no chord changes, play with a constant background drone (a Tanpura app or a simple open E-string drone). This makes the note intervals stand out.
  2. Practice Legato (Meend): Indian classical music is famous for slides and microtones. Use glissando slides and smooth hammer-ons/pull-offs to mimic the vocal-like slides (Meend) of the Sitar or Veena.

Explore the full fretboard mapping of 20+ common Hindustani and Carnatic scales using our free Scale Explorer.

title: How to Identify Chords by Ear for Guitarists
description: Master the art of transcribing music. Learn how to identify keys, recognize chord progressions, and figure out guitar chords by ear with active listening methods.
author: Abhinav Vaidya
html:
Have you ever listened to a song and wanted to play along instantly, but couldn't find the chords online? Or maybe the chords you found online just sounded... off?

Learning how to identify guitar chords by ear is one of the most liberating skills you can develop as a musician. It transforms you from someone who just reads tabs to someone who truly understands the music.

In this guide, we’ll break down the exact step-by-step process professionals use to transcribe chords by ear.

Step 1: Find the Key Center (The Root)

Before you try to guess any chord, you must find the key center of the song. The key center is the "home base"—the note where the song feels resolved and at rest.

How to do it:

  1. Hum along with the song until you find a single note that sounds like it fits over almost the entire song, especially at the very end of a chorus or verse.
  2. Pick up your guitar and find that note on the low E or A string.
  3. If that note is, say, G, then the song is likely in either G Major or G Minor.

Step 2: Listen to the Bassline

The secret weapon of transcribing chords is the bass player. In 90% of popular music, the bass guitar plays the root note of the active chord on the first beat of each measure.

If you can hear the bassline, you can figure out the chord progression:

  • Use high-quality headphones.
  • If you are struggling to hear the bass, try using our Stem Separator AI or Vocal Splitter to isolate the bass track entirely.
  • Match the bass notes to the notes on your guitar neck. If the bass plays C -> G -> A -> F, those are the root notes of your chords!

Step 3: Determine Chord Quality (Major vs. Minor)

Once you know the root notes (e.g., C, G, A, F), you need to determine if each chord is Major (happy, bright) or Minor (sad, dark).

The Simple Test:

Play the major version of the chord. If it sounds correct, you're good. If it sounds clashy or tense, try playing the minor version.

  • C Major vs. C Minor
  • G Major vs. G Minor
  • A Major vs. A Minor
  • F Major vs. F Minor

Tip: If you're stuck, you can also run the song through our Chord AI Free Engine to get a visual map of the exact chord voicings in real-time.

Step 4: Use the Roman Numeral System (Music Theory)

Music theory isn't just academic—it's a shortcut. In any given key, only certain chords naturally occur.

For example, in a Major Key, the naturally occurring chords (diatonic chords) follow this pattern:

  • I (Major)
  • ii (Minor)
  • iii (Minor)
  • IV (Major)
  • V (Major)
  • vi (Minor)
  • vii° (Diminished)

If the song is in G Major, the common chords you will encounter are:

  • G Major (I)
  • A Minor (ii)
  • B Minor (iii)
  • C Major (IV)
  • D Major (V)
  • E Minor (vi)

If you already identified the key as G Major, and you hear a chord with an E root, you can almost guarantee it’s an E minor (vi) chord!

Summary Checklist to Practice

Next time you try to learn a song, turn off the internet tab search and follow this loop:

  1. Hum the root note to find the key.
  2. Listen to the low frequencies for the bass progression.
  3. Analyze the mood (Major or Minor?).
  4. Use theory (like the Circle of Fifths) to predict what chords should come next.

Like any physical muscle, ear training takes consistency. Start with simple 3-chord pop and folk songs, and gradually work your way up to complex jazz or rock progressions!
